codeReview.summary: The feature implements the requested SAN move history, captured-piece tracking, undo/new-game flows, post-game review behavior, and engine-reset handling in the expected chess.js/useChessEngine boundaries. I found one minor UX gap in the move-history auto-scroll trigger, but no blocking correctness issues.

issues.description: The auto-scroll effect only reruns when `rows.length` changes. Black engine replies update the existing last row instead of adding a new row, so an overflowed history panel will not force-scroll to the newest move in that case even though the latest notation changed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker skill still tells workers to run frontend commands from `web/`, but this repository runs from the repo root. That can send future workers to a nonexistent directory even though `.factory/services.yaml` already documents the correct root-level commands.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/frontend-worker/SKILL.md:99,119-120 instruct `cd web && ...`; compare with .factory/services.yaml:5,30 which define `pnpm dev --turbopack --port 3001` from the repo root.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker skill does not explain what to do when the mandated dev-server port is already occupied by a healthy instance, and it does not mention deterministic finished-game setup for validating post-game flows. The worker had to improvise both behaviors during verification.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff 2026-03-31T21-38-53-142Z__website-game-features__e1c794ba-2cfc-4a12-8851-e71a331d402f.json:87-99 records deviations for reusing the healthy port-3001 server after `EADDRINUSE` and for injecting a deterministic checkmate sequence to verify post-game undo.

summary: Pass with one non-blocking issue. The feature meets the requested gameplay behavior for move history, captures, undo, new game, divergent continuations, and post-game review; the only code issue I found is that move-history auto-scroll is keyed to row-count changes instead of every latest-move update.
